title: Number of people living in extreme poverty
subtitle: Extreme poverty is defined as living below the [International Poverty Line](#dod:international-poverty-line) of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Number of people living in extreme poverty

Extreme poverty is defined as living below the International Poverty Line of $3 per day. This data is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

The stacked area chart tracks the global population distribution between those living in extreme poverty and those not, spanning the years 1990 to 2026. The vertical axis measures the number of people in billions, ranging from 0 to 9 billion, while the horizontal axis marks time in five-year increments. The data is expressed in international-$ at 2021 prices and relates to income or consumption per capita. Two distinct colored regions compose the total height of the chart: a lower dark pink area representing the population living in extreme poverty and an upper light orange area representing the population not in extreme poverty. In 1990, the dark pink region begins at a height of roughly 2.3 billion people and remains relatively flat through the mid-1990s before beginning a steady decline. By 2010, the number of people in this category drops to approximately 1.5 billion, and by 2026, the band narrows to just under 1 billion. Conversely, the light orange region expands significantly over the same period. While the total global population rises from roughly 5.3 billion in 1990 to over 8 billion in 2026, the growth occurs almost entirely within the non-poor category. The boundary between the two regions shifts downward relative to the total population size, showing that the share of people outside the poverty line increases as the absolute number of people inside it decreases. The visual pattern shows a consistent reduction in the count of people living in extreme poverty alongside a continuous rise in the count of people not living in extreme poverty.
